OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT BENEFIT COSMETICS, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE ALLEGATIONS IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 61284312) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BAINTER, AIMEE INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES PURSUANT TO THE CALIFORNIA PRIVATE ATTORNEYS GENERAL ACT.
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT BENEFIT COSMETICS LLC'S MOTIN TO STRIKE ALLEGATIONS IN PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BENEFIT COSMETICS, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE ALLEGATIONS IN PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING REIMBURSEMENT OF EXPENSES IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF AIMEE BAINTER, GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. MS. BAINTER HAS FAILED TO ALLEGED ULTIMATE FACTS SUPPORTING HER REIMBURSEMENT OF EXPENSES ALLEGATIONS. IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, SHE IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE SUCH ULTIMATE FACTS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-22-2019 Off Calendar

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act (Cal. Labor Code 2698, Et Seq.) Settlement Agreement And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs, Incentive Payments, And Administrator Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF AIMEE BAINTER, NATALIE MERCHANT's Motion For Approval Of Private Attorneys General Act (Cal. Labor Code 2698, Et Seq.) Settlement Agreement And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs, Incentive Payments, And Administrator Costs.

Plaintiffs' motion for approval of PAGA settlement and award of attorneys' fees and costs, incentive payments, and administrator costs is granted. The amended settlement agreement and release and Plaintiffs' supplemental showing address the concerns expressed by the Court in denying without prejudice the motion for approval of the prior version of the settlement agreement. A compliance date is set for March 11, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302 per the agreement of the parties, email dated 5-9-18 and approval of the Court.

Prevailing party to submit a separate proposed order and a proposed judgment.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF AIMEE BAINTER's DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT BENETTE COSMETICS LLC'S ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff Aimee Bainter's demurrer to the first, second, third, fifth and sixth affirmative defenses in the answer to the first amended complaint is overruled in its entirety. None of the affirmative defenses is uncertain and the sixth defense is adequately alleged. Any additional information about any of the defenses can be obtained in discovery.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF AIMEE BAINTER's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF DEFENDANT BENEFIT COSMETICS,LLC'S ANSWER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff Aimee Bainter's motion to strike portions of answer to the first amended complaint is denied in its entirety. This motion is a total waste of time. If defendant prevails, its entitlement to fees and costs will be determined as of the date of the judgment and it would be entitled to seek fees and costs whether or not it requests them in the answer. Defendant is also entitled to seek to amend its answer and its purported reservation is of no consequence. Motions like this only increase costs without any benefit.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-19-2018 Set For Trial On Apr-29-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Allegations In Plaintiffs Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Nov-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BENEFIT COSMETICS, LLC'S Motion To Strike Allegations In Plaintiffs Complaint is, as to the allegations regarding reimbursement of expenses in the complaint filed by plaintiff Aimee Bainter is granted with twenty days leave to amend. Ms. Bainter has failed to alleged ultimate facts supporting her reimbursement of expenses allegations. If she can do so in good faith, she is given leave to amend to allege such ultimate facts. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
